data.title: Ryan Merkel Maintains Hold on Pool as In-Line Results and Mixed Margin Trends Support Balanced Risk-Reward
data.description:
William Blair analyst Ryan Merkel has maintained their neutral stance on POOL stock, giving a Hold rating on July 20.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Ryan Merkel has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors related to Pool’s current performance and outlook. The company delivered results and guidance in line with already muted expectations, easing fears that management would sharply cut forecasts, yet not providing enough upside surprise to warrant a more positive stance.

At the same time, cost discipline and SG&A leverage are encouraging, signaling some operational improvement, but pressure on gross margins from higher freight costs and mix remains a concern. With demand trends characterized by steady maintenance activity but sluggish discretionary spending, Merkel sees a balanced risk‑reward profile, supporting a neutral, or Hold, recommendation rather than a more decisive call.

In another report released on July 20, Stifel Nicolaus also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $235.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Maintains Hold on Fiera as Net Outflows Persist and Price Target Trimmed to $6.50
data.description:
Fiera Capital A, the Financial sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Etienne Ricard from BMO Capital maintained a Hold rating on the stock and has a C$6.50 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Etienne Ricard has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to Fiera’s recent operating trends and valuation profile. The firm is facing persistent net outflows in both PineStone-related and other public market strategies, and the latest quarter showed weaker-than-expected assets under management, leading him to trim his target price from $7.00 to $6.50.

At the same time, Fiera’s shares trade at a relatively attractive free cash flow yield and the company benefits from diversified capabilities across public and private markets, which anchor downside risk and leave room for upside if flows improve. Ricard’s Hold stance reflects his view that a meaningful and sustained recovery in net inflows is necessary before the stock’s valuation can re-rate closer to peers, justifying a neutral recommendation for now.

In another report released on July 13, RBC Capital also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a C$6.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Pantginis Reiterates Buy on Nuvectis, Citing Ciprocopan Approval and Pipeline Diversification; Price Target Maintained at $39
data.description:
Joseph Pantginis, an analyst from H.C. Wainwright, reiterated the Buy rating on Nuvectis Pharma. The associated price target remains the same with $39.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Joseph Pantginis has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, primarily the clinical and regulatory validation gained from the first global approval of ciprocopan (NXP100) in China for treatment-naïve PNH. This approval, supported by a Phase 3 trial showing superior efficacy and favorable safety versus Soliris, underscores the differentiated profile of NXP100 as a once-daily oral Factor B inhibitor and strengthens confidence in Nuvectis’s ex-China development plans.

He also highlights that Nuvectis’s licensing of NXP100 and NXP200 from Haisco transforms the company from a single-asset oncology story into a more diversified, late-stage platform in both complement-mediated diseases and oncology. With additional potential catalysts including a pending China application for NXP100 in C5-experienced PNH, ongoing trials in IgAN and lupus nephritis, and early but promising data for NXP200 in multiple BRAF-mutant cancers, Pantginis sees attractive upside potential, supporting his unchanged $39 price target and Buy recommendation.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: LB Pharmaceuticals: Buy Rating Reaffirmed on LB-102 Clinical Momentum and Regulatory Progress; $45 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
In a report released today, Ram Selvaraju from H.C. Wainwright reiterated a Buy rating on LB Pharmaceuticals, Inc., with a price target of $45.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Ram Selvaraju has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to LB-102’s clinical and regulatory progress. He views the accelerated timeline for the pivotal Phase 3 NOVA-2 schizophrenia trial and the anticipated pre-NDA meeting with the FDA as materially increasing the visibility and potential speed to approval and commercialization, which supports his unchanged $45 price target.

He also highlights the robust design of NOVA-2 and the ongoing NOVA-3 long-term safety study as foundations for clinically meaningful and regulatory-grade data. In addition, he underscores LB Pharmaceuticals’ strong cash position and extended runway, which should carry the company through several key Phase 2 readouts in bipolar depression and adjunctive major depressive disorder, reinforcing confidence in LB-102’s broader therapeutic potential and the overall investment case.

In another report released yesterday, Piper Sandler also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $78.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Aurora Innovation: Buy Rating Reiterated as Driverless Miles Climb and Observer-Free Trucks Launch; Price Target Maintained at $15
data.description:
George Gianarikas, an analyst from Canaccord Genuity, maintained the Buy rating on Aurora Innovation. The associated price target remains the same with $15.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

George Gianarikas has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Aurora Innovation’s strong operational progress and confidence in its technology. He points to Aurora’s expanding record of fully driverless miles, now approaching 440,000, as tangible evidence that the Aurora Driver can safely handle real-world conditions without human intervention. This growing mileage base reinforces the view that the company’s autonomous platform is maturing toward commercial viability.

He also emphasizes the strategic importance of Aurora launching driverless trucks with International, part of TRATON, that operate with no driver and no in-cab observer. In his view, using upfitted vehicles ahead of 2027 assembly-line deployments helps accelerate adoption, address any lingering perception issues created by prior “observer” requirements, and signal to ecosystem partners and future customers that Aurora is ready for scaled, observer-free operations. With these developments, he maintains a Buy rating and keeps his price target unchanged at $15.

In another report released today, Northland Securities also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $11.00 price target.

AUR’s price has also changed moderately for the past six months – from $4.880 to $6.610, which is a 35.45% increase.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Douglas Tsao Reiterates Buy on Hansa, Maintains $103 Price Target Amid Strategic Pivot and Operational Recovery
data.description:
Douglas Tsao, an analyst from H.C. Wainwright, reiterated the Buy rating on Hansa Biopharma AB. The associated price target remains the same with SEK103.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Douglas Tsao has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including signs of operational recovery and a clearer strategic focus after the SERB transaction. Second-quarter revenues rebounded sequentially, driven by strong uptake in Spain and continued solid performance in France, Germany, Italy and the UK, reinforcing management’s view that earlier weakness was partly temporary even though near-term volatility is still expected.

The SERB deal provides upfront cash, lowers European cost of goods, and effectively extends Hansa’s financial runway while allowing management to concentrate resources on U.S. approval and launch. With an FDA decision expected in December and imlifidase positioned as a potentially standard-of-care therapy for sensitized kidney transplant patients, Tsao sees substantial long-term value despite near-term reimbursement and economic complexities. His price target remains unchanged at $103, reflecting confidence in the company’s strategic pivot and U.S. growth opportunity.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
